feedback.text: I believe that the Digital Fairness Act would indubitably be one of the most important parts that would protect consumers from unfair practices in the video game industry. Such as publishers have restrictions in place regarding predatory monetisation methods, but also the unlimited freedom in their Terms of Service to impose biased conditions that are in no favor for the consumer. One clear example that comes to mind is the fact that games sold as products force you after purchase to agree with their Terms of Service to be able to access, which can include clauses such as "the publisher can revoke access for the user to the product whenever, with or without reason". If a person purchases a product, it should be at least informed about the prospect of the time period for when that product will be available and offer options to the user for after the game becomes financially unviable for the company to support. I hope my feedback provides some clarity on this issue and will be taken into account.
